programmes.show_description: (2003) Action with Colin Farrell and Samuel L Jackson. A loose canon joins an elite force to escort a fugitive to chokey. But the baddie's put a reward out for help with his escape.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Six children battle for three places in the final by using quilling skills to upcycle a picture frame, and their creative flair to give an old chest of drawers a new lease of life.
Teleshopping.
(1956) Sci-fi thriller. Hugh Marlowe stars as a scientist who discovers Earth is threatened by a fleet of flying saucers bent on global conquest. With special effects by Ray Harryhausen.
Alexander Skarsgard, Anya Taylor-Joy and director Robert Eggers talk about making their Viking adventure.
(1971) Sci-fi thriller. When a space rocket crash-lands in the US, a fatal alien virus is released, sparking a desperate battle to defeat the extraterrestrial life form and save mankind.
(1964) Adventure with Richard Widmark as a Viking who leads his men on the quest for a huge bell made of solid gold, but with Sidney Poitier, leader of an army of Moors, on the trail too.
Alexander Skarsgard, Anya Taylor-Joy and director Robert Eggers talk about making their Viking adventure.
(1995) Romantic drama based on Arthurian legend, starring Richard Gere, Sean Connery and Julia Ormond. Can nomadic swordsman Lancelot contain his love for King Arthur's fiancée?
(2022) Epic Viking saga starring Alexander Skarsgård as a warrior prince who goes on the rampage to Iceland to avenge his murdered father. With Ethan Hawke, Nicole Kidman and Björk.
(1999) Darkly comic horror with Guy Pearce and Robert Carlyle. In 1840s California, an American soldier with a taste for human flesh struggles to stay clean in the face of bad influence.
(2005) Violent action-thriller starring Jason Statham as the tough courier Frank Martin, who's hired to guard a government drugs czar's son. But his mission soon lurches towards disaster.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1955) Romantic drama with Jane Wyman and Rock Hudson. A wealthy widow decides to restart her life. But her children and her social set disapprove of her lover, forcing her to rethink.
(1957) Second World War drama. Deborah Kerr plays a nun stranded on a Japanese-held Pacific island, with an untalkative marine (Robert Mitchum) as her only companion and hope of salvation.

programmes.show_name: The True Story of Jesse James
programmes.show_description: (1957) Robert Wagner stars as the infamous outlaw in Nicholas Ray's western, which follows the last 18 years of the robber's life.
(1947) Supernatural romance starring Rex Harrison as the spirit of a gruff sea captain who haunts widow Gene Tierney, only for her to fall in love with him. Directed by Joseph L Mankiewicz.
(2019) Will Smith stars in Ang Lee's action-thriller as both an over-the-hill hitman and the young clone of himself sent to take him out. With Clive Owen.
(2023) Molly Gordon and Nick Lieberman's fabulously quirky mockumentary about a kids theater (sic) where it's difficult to tell who the adults are and where bad acting is a given.
(2002) Action thriller starring Vin Diesel as Xander Cage: a thrill-seeking extreme sports enthusiast, stuntman and rebellious athlete-turned-reluctant spy. With Samuel L Jackson.
